title: Live Virus SARS-CoV-2 - iPSC Pneumocyte - Antiviral Screening
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bc6ki","text":"The following is a protocol for live virus antiviral screening against SARS-CoV-2 in iPSC pneumocyte cells. iPSC pneumocytes are prophylactically treated with antiviral candidates that have been serially diluted to investigate activity, via IC50 values generated via analysis of immunofluorescent staining, against SARS-CoV-2. Each live virus screening assay is accompanied by a matching cytotoxicity screening assay to investigate drug toxicity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4tbhl","text":"Always wear appropriate PPE for this protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2m13a","text":"Refer to Material Safety Data Sheets for additional safety and handling information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bv3v3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bv6dt","text":"10% Media","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7t8b5","text":"2% Media","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"11cud","text":"96-well plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdbp8","text":"96-well deep well plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3eecf","text":"12-channel 200/300ul multichannel pipette","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d8itt","text":"200/300ul filtered tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ml95","text":"Compounds of interest","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5s3f3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1r3mm","text":"Virus(es) of interest","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"crvlv","text":"PPE","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6dmgh","text":"reservoir","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Obtain pneumocyte cells seeded in 96-well plates and appropriate media. Total amount of plates should be divided in half - one for antiviral screening and one for cytotoxicity screening. iPSC Pneumocyte Live Virus SARS-CoV-2 Cytotoxicity Screening Assay by Briana L McGovern For each plate you can test 3 compounds (in triplicate, 8 dilutions per compound), 2 DMSO controls, and 1 uninfected control in the following layout: Treatment layout

The serial dilutions will follow different protocols depending on if you will do them by hand or by using a TecanD300e. Before the assay, you should supplement the provided pneumocyte media with DMSO for your screening. DMSO supplementation will correspond to the highest amount of drug stock used.

Prepare the Deep Well Plate (96 well) Thermo Fisher Catalog #10045 according to this diagram: Deep well set up for manual serial dilutions The first row will be the [max] of your compounds. This row will contain 1100ul of 2% media without DMSO. The media for this row does not receive any DMSO since you will add drug dissolved in DMSO directly to it. The remaining rows of the plate will be filled with 750ul 2% media + supplemented with DMSO These volumes are enough to treat both the antiviral and cytotoxicity plates.
After approximately 01:30:00 you should begin prepping to head up to the BSL-3 facility, where you will infect the plates with SARS-CoV-2.
Incubate the cells at 37 °C 5% CO2 for 02:00:00 . This pretreatment time frame could differ depending on what sort of compounds you'd like to test.
Calculate the dilution of the virus(es) of interest and bring the calculations with you. Calculating Multiplicity of Infection (MOI) by Briana L McGovern

After 24 hours, add 100ul 1 % (v/v) Formaldehyde Contributed by users to all wells on the plate. Spray each lid gently with ethanol and replace. Double bag the plates in red biohazard bags to bring them out of the facility.
Incubate for 24:00:00 at room temperature inside the bags.

Image the infected plates on the Cytation1 (Imager, Biotek, n/a, https://www.agilent.com/en/product/cell-analysis/cell-imaging-microscopy/cell-imaging-multimode-readers/biotek-cytation-1-cell-imaging-multimode-reader-1623200 )
After approximately 01:30:00 you should begin prepping to head up to the BSL-3 facility, where you will infect the plates with SARS-CoV-2.
Calculate the dilution of the virus(es) of interest. Calculating Multiplicity of Infection (MOI) by Briana L McGovern
Retrieve your virus(es) from the -80. Thaw your virus(es) and create your dilution(s)
Using the reservoir, infect your plates (except the uninfected control column) with 50ul of the virus dilution.
Incubate at 37 °C for 24:00:00
Add 100ul 10% Formaldehyde to all wells on the plate. Spray each lid gently with ethanol and replace. Double bag the plates in red biohazard bags to bring them out of the facility.
Incubate for 24:00:00 at room temperature inside the bags.

Create a screening priority list ahead of time. This list will have the compounds of interest, what maximum concentration you'd like to screen at, and where the compounds are located in storage. We recommend organizing the compounds the day before. These conditions will be different depending on if you are going to perform the serial dilutions manually, or using the Tecan D300e (which is essentially a drug printer). The protocol will fork into these two methods below.
Perform the serial dilutions: Add the compounds of interest, based on your testing plan, to the first row of the deep well.
mix well and move 375ul from row 1 to the row 2. This is your first 1:3 dilution. Schematic of the manual serial dilutions
Repeat until the entire deep well has been done. You now have 8 concentrations of your compounds of interest.

Remove the growth media, by hand, from the plates. The pneumocytes are very sensitive so it's best to aspirate the media by hand rather than vacuum line. Work one pair at a time to prevent drying of the cells. Add 100ul of the serial dilutions to the wells in the following set up: You will perform this for both the infection plate and the matching cytotoxicity plate Treatment layout You should always have the plates in the correct orientation (top left corner should be A1) so that you are sure where the highest and lowest concentrations of compound are.
Remove your compounds from -20 to thaw while you set up.
Remove your compounds from -20 to thaw while you set up.
Prepare the Deep Well Plate (96 well) Thermo Fisher Catalog #10045 Add 800ul 2% Media to the first row (A) Add 800ul 2% Media supplemented with DMSO to the rest of the rows
You will not be performing any serial dilutions, the tecan will dispense the appropriate amount of drug into each well for you once you set it up. Using the Tecan D300e by Briana L McGovern

Image the infected plates on the Cytation1 (Imager, Biotek, n/a, https://www.agilent.com/en/product/cell-analysis/cell-imaging-microscopy/cell-imaging-multimode-readers/biotek-cytation-1-cell-imaging-multimode-reader-1623200 )
Go to the BSL-3 facility. Perform the entering procedures.
Retrieve your virus(es) from the -80. Thaw your virus(es) and create your dilution(s)
Incubate at 37 °C for 24:00:00
In parallel, process the cytotoxicity plates. iPSC Pneumocyte Live Virus SARS-CoV-2 Cytotoxicity Screening Assay by Briana L McGovern
Follow the SARS-CoV-2 staining protocol SARS-CoV-2 Antiviral Immunofluorescence staining Protocol by Briana L McGovern
Treat the plates with 100ul compound using the deep wells.
Incubate for 02:00:00 at 37 °C 5%CO2
Go to the BSL-3. Perform the entering procedures.
Generating Antiviral & Cytotoxicity Curves by Briana L McGovern
SARS-CoV-2 Antiviral Immunofluorescence staining Protocol by Briana L McGovern
Prepare your materials for entering the facility. Tips, media, PPE etc.

Before heading up to infect the plates, you should perform the mock infection on the cytoxicity plates . We want both plates to match so that we can compare them later. Add 50ul 2% media to the wells. iPSC Pneumocyte Live Virus SARS-CoV-2 Cytotoxicity Screening Assay by Briana L McGovern
Using the reservoir, infect your plates (except the uninfected control column) with 50ul of the virus dilution.
Generating Antiviral & Cytotoxicity Curves by Briana L McGovern
Follow the cyotoxicity protocol for the uninfected plates iPSC Pneumocyte Live Virus SARS-CoV-2 Cytotoxicity Screening Assay by Briana L McGovern
creator: Briana L McGovern
